summary: Feature request to expose background blur (backdrop filter) via SkCanvas::SaveLayerRec; the requested SKCanvasSaveLayerRec.Backdrop API is already present in SkiaSharp, making this request largely fulfilled.

reproEvidence.environmentDetails: No specific platform mentioned; cross-platform feature request filed 2021-09-14

repoLinks.description: WebKit backdrop-filter examples the reporter wants to replicate
Upstream Skia issue reporter referenced as the solution path
Skia SaveLayerRec docs – fBackdrop is the backdrop image filter field
PR #2962 referenced in comments as likely dependent on / related to this issue
Issue #3621 tracks SKCanvasSaveLayerRec.BackdropTileMode (Skia m131) as an incomplete binding

fixStatus.reason: SKCanvasSaveLayerRec.Backdrop (mapped to SkCanvas::SaveLayerRec::fBackdrop) already exists in binding/SkiaSharp/SKCanvas.cs. Users can pass an SKImageFilter.CreateBlur() as the Backdrop field to achieve backdrop blur today. The only missing piece is BackdropTileMode (Skia m131), which is tracked in #3621.

analysis.summary: The core feature — backdrop blur via SKCanvasSaveLayerRec — is already implemented. SKCanvasSaveLayerRec.Backdrop accepts any SKImageFilter (including CreateBlur), which is the mechanism Skia exposes for backdrop/frosted-glass effects. The newer BackdropTileMode field (added in Skia m131 to control edge tiling) is not yet bound and is tracked separately in #3621.
analysis.rationale: Reporter asked for exposure of SkCanvas::SaveLayerRec fBackdrop. Code investigation confirms this field is already bound as SKCanvasSaveLayerRec.Backdrop. The fix status is high-confidence because the test SaveLayerRecWithImageFilterIsCorrect demonstrates the same mechanism. The only missing piece is BackdropTileMode (m131), which is a separate enhancement.

codeInvestigation.finding: SKCanvasSaveLayerRec struct has Backdrop property (type SKImageFilter?) that maps to fBackdrop in the native SkCanvas::SaveLayerRec. Already fully bound and usable.
SKImageFilter.CreateBlur(sigmaX, sigmaY, tileMode) API exists with multiple overloads — can be passed directly as the Backdrop field to achieve background blur.
SaveLayerRecWithImageFilterIsCorrect test demonstrates setting Backdrop on SKCanvasSaveLayerRec with an image filter and calling canvas.SaveLayer(rec) — confirms the API works end-to-end.

SKCanvasSaveLayerRecNative struct has fBackdrop field (sk_imagefilter_t). The field is included in ToNative() conversion. No BackdropTileMode field present — confirms that enhancement is still missing.

keySignals.text: Skia has this method https://api.skia.org/structSkCanvas_1_1SaveLayerRec.html — Can SkiaSharp implement it?
keySignals.interpretation: Reporter specifically requests the SaveLayerRec.fBackdrop exposure, which is already done.
PR #2962 likely adds BackdropTileMode, the remaining missing enhancement.

keySignals.text: SKCanvasSaveLayerRec.BackdropTileMode | m131 | ⚠️ Incomplete | Struct needs new field
keySignals.interpretation: Confirms core Backdrop is there but the newer tile-mode field is still pending.

workarounds: Use SKCanvasSaveLayerRec.Backdrop = SKImageFilter.CreateBlur(sigmaX, sigmaY) with canvas.SaveLayer(rec) to apply a backdrop blur effect today.
For shader-based blur alternative, use SKRuntimeEffect with a custom blur shader passed as a backdrop filter.

resolution.hypothesis: The reporter's request is fulfilled by SKCanvasSaveLayerRec.Backdrop, which already exposes the Skia fBackdrop field. A code example was never posted, so the reporter may be unaware.

proposals.title: Post working code example and close
proposals.description: Reply with a code snippet showing how to use SKCanvasSaveLayerRec.Backdrop = SKImageFilter.CreateBlur(...) and reference #3621 for the pending BackdropTileMode enhancement.

resolution.recommendedProposal: Post working code example and close
resolution.recommendedReason: Core feature is already implemented. A clear code example and close-as-fixed is the most helpful response. BackdropTileMode remains tracked in #3621.

actionability.reason: SKCanvasSaveLayerRec.Backdrop API is already present and enables backdrop blur. The issue can be closed with a comment showing the usage pattern. The newer BackdropTileMode field is tracked separately in #3621.

actions.description: Apply feature-request and core area labels
